A: The first letter of the English alphabet.
a: The indefinite article used before words starting with a consonant sound.
about: Concerning or on the subject of; approximately.
above: At a higher position or level than something else.
action: The process of doing something to achieve a goal.
activity: Something you do, especially for enjoyment or a purpose.
actor: A person who performs in plays, films, or television shows.
add: To put something together with something else to increase the total.
address: The details of where a person lives or an organization is located.
afraid: Feeling fear or worry about something that might happen.
after: Following in time, position, or order.
afternoon: The part of the day from noon until evening.
again: One more time; returning to a previous state.
age: The number of years a person has lived or a thing has existed.
ago: In the past, before the present moment.
agree: To have the same opinion as someone or accept a proposal.
airport: A place where aircraft take off and land, with passenger facilities.
album: A collection of music recordings released together; a book for photos.
all: The whole amount, number, or extent of something.
almost: Very nearly but not quite; just short of something.
along: Moving in a line beside something; in addition.
already: Before a specified time; earlier than expected.
always: At all times; on every occasion.
am: First person singular present tense of the verb 'be'.
an: The indefinite article used before words starting with a vowel sound.
and: A conjunction connecting words, phrases, or clauses together.
animal: A living creature that is not a plant, especially one that moves.
another: One more of the same kind; a different one.
answer: A reply to a question or solution to a problem.
any: One or some, regardless of quantity; used in questions and negatives.
anybody: Any person; used in questions, negatives, and conditionals.
anyone: Any person at all; used in questions and negative sentences.
anything: Any thing or matter, regardless of what it is.
apple: A round fruit with red, green, or yellow skin and sweet or tart flesh.
april: The fourth month of the year, with 30 days.
apron: A garment worn over clothes to protect them while cooking or working.
are: Second person singular and all plural forms of the verb 'be'.
arm: The upper limb of the human body, from shoulder to hand.
around: On all sides of something; in the surrounding area; approximately.
arrive: To reach a destination after traveling.
art: Creative works such as paintings, drawings, and sculptures.
article: A piece of writing in a newspaper or magazine; a grammatical word like 'a' or 'the'.
as: Used to indicate how something is done; at the same time as.
ask: To put a question to someone; to request something.
at: Used to indicate location, time, or direction toward a point.
august: The eighth month of the year, with 31 days.
aunt: Your parent's sister, or your uncle's wife.
autumn: The season between summer and winter when leaves fall from trees.
awake: Not sleeping; in a conscious state.
away: At a distance from a place or person; to another location.
B: The second letter of the English alphabet.
baby: A very young child, especially one under one year old.
back: The rear side of something; to return to a previous place.
bad: Of poor quality; unpleasant or harmful.
bag: A flexible container made of cloth, paper, or plastic for carrying things.
ball: A round object used in sports and games.
banana: A long curved yellow fruit that grows in tropical regions.
band: A group of musicians; a strip of material worn around something.
bank: A financial institution where money is kept; the land beside a river.
bar: A place that serves drinks; a solid block of material.
baseball: An American team sport played with a bat and ball on a diamond-shaped field.
basketball: A sport where two teams try to throw a ball through a hoop high above the floor.
bat: A wooden or metal club for hitting a ball; a flying nocturnal mammal.
bath: The act of washing your whole body in water; a bathtub.
bathroom: A room with a toilet and facilities for washing.
be: A verb used to indicate existence or a state of being.
beach: An area of sand or pebbles beside the sea or a lake.
bean: A small seed from a pod plant, used as food.
bear: A large heavy wild animal with thick fur; to tolerate or carry something.
beautiful: Pleasing to the senses or mind; very attractive.
became: Past tense of 'become': changed into or started being something.
because: For the reason that; as a result of.
become: To start being something different; to grow into.
bed: A piece of furniture for sleeping on.
bedroom: A room in a home used for sleeping.
bee: A small flying insect that makes honey and pollinates flowers.
beef: The meat from a cow, used as food.
before: At an earlier time; in front of something.
began: Past tense of 'begin': started doing something.
begin: To start doing something or to come into existence.
behind: At or to the back of something; following.
believe: To think something is true or real; to have faith in something.
bell: A hollow metal object that rings when struck, used as a signal.
below: At a lower level or position than something else.
beside: Next to or at the side of something.
best: Of the highest quality; most effective or suitable.
better: Of a higher quality or standard; more favorable.
between: In the space separating two things; at an intermediate point.
bicycle: A two-wheeled vehicle powered by pedaling.
big: Of large size or extent; important.
bike: Informal for bicycle; a two-wheeled pedal-powered vehicle.
bird: A feathered animal with wings that typically can fly.
birth: The event of being born; the beginning of life outside the womb.
birthday: The anniversary of the day a person was born.
biscuit: A small baked bread or cookie, depending on regional usage.
black: The darkest color, absorbing all light; the opposite of white.
blank: Empty with nothing written or printed; a space to fill in.
block: A solid piece of material; a section of buildings between streets.
blow: To move air from your mouth; a hard hit or strike.
blue: The color of a clear sky or the sea.
board: A flat piece of wood or other material; to get onto a vehicle.
boat: A small watercraft used to travel on water such as rivers and lakes.
body: The physical structure of a person or animal.
bone: A hard piece of the skeleton inside the body.
book: A written or printed work bound in covers; to reserve something in advance.
borrow: To take something temporarily with the intention of returning it.
both: Two things or people together; each of two.
bottle: A container with a narrow neck for holding liquids.
bottom: The lowest part or surface of something.
bought: Past tense and past participle of 'buy': purchased something.
bowl: A round deep dish for food or liquids.
box: A square or rectangular container with flat sides.
boy: A male child or young man.
brain: The organ inside the skull that controls thinking and feeling.
bread: A baked food made from flour, water, and yeast.
break: To separate into pieces; a pause or rest period.
breakfast: The first meal of the day, eaten in the morning.
breathe: To take air into the lungs and let it out.
bridge: A structure built to cross a river, road, or other gap.
bright: Giving off a lot of light; vivid in color; intelligent.
bring: To carry or take something or someone to a place.
broke: Past tense of 'break': separated something into pieces.
broken: Damaged and no longer working properly; past participle of break.
brother: A male sibling; a boy or man with the same parents.
brought: Past tense and past participle of 'bring': carried to a place.
brown: The color of wood or earth; a mix of red, yellow, and black.
brush: A tool with bristles for cleaning or painting; to clean with a brush.
bucket: A cylindrical container with a handle for carrying liquids.
build: To construct something by putting materials together.
building: A structure with walls and a roof, such as a house or office.
built: Past tense and past participle of 'build': constructed something.
burger: A cooked patty of minced meat served in a bun.
bus: A large road vehicle that carries many passengers on a fixed route.
business: The activity of buying and selling goods or services; a company.
busy: Actively engaged in work or an activity; not free.
but: Used to introduce something contrasting or unexpected.
butter: A yellow fatty food made from cream, spread on bread.
butterfly: An insect with large colorful wings that develops from a caterpillar.
button: A small disc sewn onto clothing to fasten it; a switch you press.
buy: To get something by paying money for it.
by: Indicating who did something; near; through the means of.
bye: An informal way of saying goodbye.
C: The third letter of the English alphabet.
cake: A sweet baked food made with flour, eggs, and sugar.
call: To speak to someone on the phone; to give a name to something; to shout.
came: Past tense of 'come': moved toward a place.
camera: A device for taking photographs or recording video.
camp: An outdoor place where people stay in tents; to sleep outdoors.
can: To be able to do something; a metal container for food or drink.
can't: Short form of 'cannot'; unable to do something.
candy: A sweet food made with sugar, such as chocolate or hard sweets.
cap: A soft hat without a brim; a covering for the top of something.
car: A four-wheeled motor vehicle for transporting people.
card: A small flat piece of stiff paper used to send messages or make payments.
care: Attention and concern for something; to feel interested or concerned.
careful: Giving close attention to avoid mistakes or danger.
carefully: In a way that avoids mistakes or dangers; attentively.
carry: To hold something and take it from one place to another.
cartoon: An animated film or a humorous drawing in a newspaper.
case: A particular situation or example; a container for protecting things.
cat: A small furry domesticated animal kept as a pet.
catch: To grab something moving; to get or contract an illness.
caught: Past tense and past participle of 'catch': grabbed something moving.
cd: A compact disc used for storing music or computer data.
celebrate: To do something enjoyable on a special occasion.
celebration: A party or event to mark a special occasion.
chair: A separate seat with a back, designed for one person.
change: To become different; money returned after paying more than needed.
character: The qualities that make a person or thing distinctive; a person in a story.
check: To examine something to confirm it is correct or safe; a written payment order.
cheese: A solid food made from milk, with many varieties and flavors.
chicken: A common farm bird kept for eggs and meat; the meat of this bird.
child: A young human being who is not yet an adult.
children: Plural of 'child': young human beings, more than one.
chocolate: A sweet food or drink made from roasted cacao beans.
choose: To select one thing from several options.
chose: Past tense of 'choose': selected from options.
chosen: Past participle of 'choose': selected as the best option.
church: A building used for Christian worship.
cinema: A place where films are shown on a large screen for an audience.
circle: A perfectly round flat shape with all points equally distant from the center.
city: A large important town with many people and buildings.
class: A group of students taught together; a lesson or period of teaching.
classmate: A person who is in the same class at school.
classroom: A room in a school where lessons take place.
clean: Free from dirt or pollution; to remove dirt from something.
clever: Quick to understand and learn; showing intelligence.
climb: To go up something using your hands and feet.
clock: A device for measuring and showing the time.
close: Near in distance or time; to shut something.
cloth: Woven or knitted fabric used for making clothes or other items.
clothes: Items worn on the body, such as shirts, trousers, and dresses.
cloud: A visible mass of water vapor floating in the sky.
club: An organization for people with a shared interest; a place for dancing.
coach: A person who trains athletes or gives instruction; a large bus.
coat: An outer garment with sleeves worn over other clothes for warmth.
code: A system of symbols or rules; a set of instructions for a computer.
coffee: A dark hot drink made from roasted coffee beans.
coke: Short for Coca-Cola; a carbonated soft drink.
cold: Low in temperature; an illness causing a runny nose and sore throat.
collect: To gather things together; to pick up items over time.
collection: A group of similar things gathered together.
college: An educational institution providing higher education after high school.
come: To move toward a place or person.
computer: An electronic device for storing and processing information.
concert: A live musical performance in front of an audience.
contest: A competition where people try to win by being best at something.
conversation: A talk between two or more people.
cook: To prepare food by heating it; a person who prepares food.
cookie: A small flat sweet baked biscuit.
cool: Slightly cold; fashionable or impressive; to become colder.
cop: Informal word for a police officer.
copy: An exact duplicate of something; to make a duplicate.
corn: A tall cereal plant with large yellow kernels on a cob.
corner: The point where two edges or sides meet; the place where streets intersect.
correct: Free from error; accurately following rules.
couch: A long comfortable piece of furniture for sitting or lying on.
could: Past form of 'can'; used to express possibility or polite requests.
could've: Short form of 'could have'; used to talk about past possibilities.
couldn't: Short form of 'could not'; was unable to.
course: A series of lessons on a subject; a part of a meal.
cousin: The child of your aunt or uncle.
cover: To place something over another to protect it; the outer layer.
cow: A large farm animal kept for milk and beef.
cream: The thick white fat-rich part of milk; a light yellowish-white color.
cry: To shed tears because of sadness or pain; to shout.
culture: The customs, arts, and beliefs shared by a group of people.
cup: A small container with a handle for drinking hot drinks.
cut: To divide or separate something with a blade.
cute: Attractive and charming, especially in a small or young way.
D: The fourth letter of the English alphabet.
daddy: An affectionate word for father, used especially by children.
dance: To move rhythmically to music; a set of choreographed movements.
dancing: The activity of moving rhythmically to music.
dark: With little or no light; having a deep shade of color.
date: A specific day on a calendar; a romantic social meeting; a sweet brown fruit.
daughter: A female child in relation to her parents.
day: A period of 24 hours; the part of the day when it is light.
dear: Beloved; used as an affectionate form of address.
december: The twelfth and last month of the year, with 31 days.
delicious: Having a very pleasant taste or smell.
describe: To give a detailed account of what something is like.
design: A plan or drawing for making something; to plan and create something.
desk: A flat-topped piece of furniture used for reading or writing.
dictionary: A book that lists words alphabetically with their meanings.
did: Past tense form of 'do'; used to form past tense questions and negatives.
didn't: Short form of 'did not'; did not do something.
difference: The way in which two things are not the same.
different: Not the same as another thing; unlike.
difficult: Needing much effort or skill; not easy.
dig: To break up and move soil or ground.
dinner: The main meal of the day, usually eaten in the evening.
dirty: Not clean; covered in dirt or marks.
discuss: To talk about a topic with others to share ideas.
dish: A flat container for food; a particular type of food prepared as a meal.
do: To perform an action or carry out a task.
does: Third person singular present of 'do'; used with he, she, it.
dog: A domestic animal kept as a pet or for work, known for loyalty.
doing: The present participle of 'do'; currently performing an action.
doll: A small figure of a person, used as a toy.
dollar: The basic unit of currency in the USA, Canada, and other countries.
door: A hinged barrier that opens and closes an entrance or exit.
down: In the direction of lower position; along or through.
drama: A play or story performed on stage or screen; an exciting event.
drank: Past tense of 'drink': swallowed a liquid.
draw: To make a picture with a pen or pencil; to pull something.
drawn: Past participle of 'draw': made a picture; pulled toward something.
dream: A series of images experienced during sleep; a strong ambition.
dress: A garment worn by women covering the body; to put on clothes.
drew: Past tense of 'draw': made a picture or pulled something.
drink: A liquid you swallow; to swallow a liquid.
drive: To operate a motor vehicle; a journey by car.
driven: Past participle of 'drive': operated a vehicle.
driver: A person who operates a motor vehicle.
drop: A small amount of liquid; to let something fall.
drove: Past tense of 'drive': operated a motor vehicle.
drum: A percussion instrument struck to make a rhythmic sound.
drunk: Past participle of 'drink': having swallowed alcohol; intoxicated.
dry: Free from moisture or water; to remove moisture.
due: Expected to happen or arrive at a certain time; owed.
dvd: A digital disc used for storing video or computer data.
E: The fifth letter of the English alphabet.
each: Every individual one of a group, considered separately.
ear: The organ used for hearing, located on the side of the head.
early: Before the expected or usual time; near the beginning.
eat: To put food in your mouth, chew, and swallow it.
egg: An oval object with a hard shell produced by birds, used as food.
either: One or the other of two options.
elementary: At the most basic level; of primary school age.
end: The final point or conclusion of something; to stop or finish.
engineer: A person who designs or builds machines, roads, or structures.
enjoy: To take pleasure from an activity or experience.
evening: The part of the day between the afternoon and night.
event: Something that happens, especially something planned and organized.
every: Each individual thing in a group without exception.
everybody: Every person in a group; all people.
everyday: Relating to ordinary daily life; happening every day.
everyone: All the people in a group; every person.
everything: All things; the whole of something.
example: Something used to illustrate a general rule or principle.
excellent: Extremely good; of very high quality.
excited: Feeling great enthusiasm and eager anticipation.
excuse: A reason given to justify something; to forgive or pardon.
exercise: Physical activity for health; practice to improve a skill.
eye: The organ of sight on the face.
F: The sixth letter of the English alphabet.
face: The front part of the head where the eyes, nose, and mouth are.
factory: A building where goods are manufactured in large quantities.
fair: Treating people equally; a public event with entertainment and stalls.
fairy: A tiny imaginary being with wings that has magical powers.
fallen: Past participle of 'fall': dropped from a higher position.
family: A group of people related by blood or marriage.
famous: Known and recognized by many people.
fan: A person who greatly admires someone or something; a device for moving air.
farm: A piece of land used to grow crops or raise animals.
farmer: A person who owns or works on a farm.
fast: Moving quickly; at high speed; to stop eating for a period.
fat: Having too much flesh on the body; a substance in food providing energy.
february: The second month of the year, with 28 or 29 days.
feed: To give food to a person or animal.
feel: To experience a physical or emotional sensation; to touch something.
feeling: An emotion or physical sensation experienced.
feet: Plural of 'foot': the parts of the body at the end of the legs.
fell: Past tense of 'fall': dropped or came down from a higher position.
felt: Past tense and past participle of 'feel': experienced a sensation.
festival: A celebration or special event, often involving music and food.
fever: A body temperature higher than normal, often a sign of illness.
field: An open area of land; a subject of study or activity.
fight: A physical struggle between people; to use force against someone.
file: A folder holding documents; a computer file storing data.
fill: To put something into a container until it is full.
find: To discover something by searching or by chance.
fine: Of acceptable quality; well in health; a penalty payment.
finish: To bring something to an end; to complete a task.
fire: The process of burning, producing heat and light; to dismiss from a job.
first: Coming before all others in time, order, or importance.
fish: A water animal with fins and scales; to try to catch fish.
fishing: The activity of trying to catch fish.
flag: A piece of cloth with a pattern, used as a symbol of a country.
flat: Smooth and level with no raised areas; an apartment.
flew: Past tense of 'fly': moved through the air.
floor: The lower surface of a room that you walk on.
flower: The colorful part of a plant that produces seeds.
flown: Past participle of 'fly': traveled through the air.
fly: To move through the air; a small insect with wings.
focus: To concentrate attention on something specific.
foggy: Covered with fog; difficult to see clearly due to mist.
following: Coming after; the people or things that come after.
food: Any substance eaten to provide nutrition and energy.
football: A sport where two teams kick a ball to score goals; the ball used.
for: Intended to belong to or benefit someone; because of; during a time period.
foreign: From, located in, or characteristic of another country.
forgot: Past tense of 'forget': failed to remember.
form: The shape or structure of something; a document with spaces to fill in.
free: Not costing anything; not restricted; able to act as you choose.
friday: The fifth day of the working week, coming before Saturday.
friend: A person you know well and like spending time with.
frog: A small amphibian with smooth skin and long legs for jumping.
from: Indicating origin, starting point, or source.
front: The side or part that faces forward; the most important side.
fruit: The sweet edible produce of a plant, containing seeds.
full: Containing as much as possible; having eaten enough.
future: The time that is yet to come; what will happen later.
